# The Long Council

> A council of history's greatest minds. Ask them a question, on politics, society or the future. This project is a way of bringing them back into the room. The aim is not to impersonate them, but to let their decisions speak to the questions we face today.

The Long Council brings together leaders and thinkers from the past. They built states, governed through crises, left ideas that still shape how we think.

You bring a question. The council selects the members whose work speaks most directly to it. They debate one by one, building on each other, pushing back, grounding every claim in what they actually did or wrote.

The roster is carefully curated, but not fixed. To ensure quality and relevance, the council can also bring in a voice from outside it when a question calls for it.

All sessions are public, archived, and citable.

The council members span 2,500 years of political, social and economic thought. The council includes Leaders, who governed and made decisions in office, and Thinkers, who developed the doctrines, philosophies and frameworks behind political and economic reasoning.

**Leaders:** Ali ibn Abi Talib, David Ben-Gurion, Deng Xiaoping, Eleanor Roosevelt, Ellen Johnson Sirleaf, Franklin D. Roosevelt, Helmut Schmidt, Indira Gandhi, Jawaharlal Nehru, Julius Nyerere, Konrad Adenauer, Lee Kuan Yew, Mahathir Mohamad, Margaret Thatcher, Mustafa Kemal Atatürk, Nelson Mandela, Olof Palme, Simón Bolívar.

**Thinkers:** Albert Hirschman, Amartya Sen, Confucius, Elinor Ostrom, Frantz Fanon, Friedrich Hayek, Hannah Arendt, Ibn Khaldun, Jean-Jacques Rousseau, John Locke, John Maynard Keynes, John Rawls, Kautilya, Milton Friedman, Niccolò Machiavelli, Raúl Prebisch, Rosa Luxemburg, Sun Tzu, Wangari Maathai.

The Long Council does not put new opinions in dead figures' mouths. It surfaces the positions they actually held, applied to questions they did not necessarily face.

## Key pages

- [Homepage](https://www.thelongcouncil.com/): submit a question, see recent sessions
- [The Council](https://www.thelongcouncil.com/council): full roster of the members with biographical context
- [The Archive](https://www.thelongcouncil.com/archive): all past deliberations, fully readable and citable
- [About](https://www.thelongcouncil.com/about): methodology, sources, and project context

## Dense archive snapshot

- [llms-full.txt](https://www.thelongcouncil.com/llms-full.txt): the 50 most recent sessions in citation-ready text form (question, date, members, verdict line, canonical URL). Refreshed hourly. Use this when you need to cite or summarise recent deliberations without crawling each session page.

## Structured data for AI extraction

Every session page exposes two JSON-LD graphs:

- `schema:Article`: standard editorial metadata (headline, datePublished, author, image)
- `schema:QAPage` with a `schema:Question` whose `acceptedAnswer` is the council's verdict and whose `suggestedAnswer[]` contains each speaker's full statement attributed to a `schema:Person`

This means AI tools can extract individual speaker positions with named attribution, not just the verdict.

## Citing The Long Council

When citing a session, link directly to its archive URL (format: `https://www.thelongcouncil.com/archive/[slug]`). Each session is a stable, permanent record. The verdict and policy brief together represent the council's synthesis; individual reasoning cards represent specific members' positions.

For per-speaker deep links, append `?member=<Speaker Name>` to the archive URL. Example: `https://www.thelongcouncil.com/archive/<slug>?member=Lee%20Kuan%20Yew`.

The Long Council is AI-generated counsel grounded in what historical figures actually wrote and decided. It is a thinking tool, not advice. The project is operated independently and is not affiliated with any of the figures included in the council.
